zh.Hamstall Ridware, Staffordshire, England: 英國斯塔福爾哈姆斯塔爾 利德瓦雷
zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Richard Cotton於1408年出生在英國斯塔福爾哈姆斯塔爾 利德瓦雷時，他的父親John COTTON I26歲，母親Isabella Fauconer23歲。他於1431年與Baroness Joan De Venables在英國英格蘭斯塔福爾結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1455年5月22日在英國賀特福爾聖 阿爾班斯享年47歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Richard Cotton于1408年出生在英国斯塔福爾哈姆斯塔爾 利德瓦雷时，他的父亲John COTTON I26岁，母亲Isabella Fauconer23岁。他于1431年与Baroness Joan De Venables在英国英格兰斯塔福爾结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1455年5月22日在英国賀特福爾聖 阿爾班斯享年47岁。

nameMeanings.name: Cotton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of numerous places called from Old English (æt) cotum (dative plural of cot) ‘at the cottages or huts’ (or sometimes possibly from a Middle English plural, coten). Examples include Coton (Cambridgeshire, Northamptonshire, Staffordshire), Cottam (East Yorkshire, Lancashire, Nottinghamshire), and Cotham (Nottinghamshire). Particularly common in Staffordshire.
French: from a pet form of the personal name Nicolas (see Nicholas ), derived via Nicot, or of the personal name Jacques , derived via Jacot.
History: John Cotton (1584–1652) was a noted Puritan preacher, who landed at Boston, MA, from London in 1633 and became leader of the Congregationalists in America.

variants.name: Cotten
meanings: Altered form of German Kotten, a habitational name from any of several places so named in Rhineland, Westphalia, Silesia, etc., or perhaps an American shortened form of composite German surnames such as Kottenhagen, Kottenhoff, Kottenkamp (see Koth ).

variants.name: Nicholas
meanings: English (southwestern England and south Wales) and Dutch: from the personal name Nicholas (from Latin Nicolaus, from Greek Nikolaos, from nikān ‘to conquer’ + laos ‘people’). Forms with -ch- are due to hypercorrection (compare Anthony ). The name in various vernacular forms was popular among Christians throughout Europe in the Middle Ages, largely as a result of the fame of a 4th-century Lycian bishop, about whom a large number of legends grew up, and who was venerated in the Orthodox Church as well as the Catholic. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates from other languages and also their patronymics and other derivatives, e.g. Croatian and Serbian Nikolić (see Nikolic ), Greek Papanikolaou ‘(son of) Nicholas the priest’ and Nikolopoulos . Compare Nickolas .

History: The colonial official and revolutionary patriot Robert Carter Nicholas was from a prominent VA family on both sides. His father was a British navy surgeon who emigrated c. 1700 from Lancashire, England, to Williamsburg, VA.

variants.name: Cotham
meanings: English (Lancashire and Yorkshire): variant of   Cotton  .

variants.name: Cottam
meanings: English (Midlands and northern England): variant of   Cotton  .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Richard Cotton was born in 1408, in Hamstall Ridware, Staffordshire, England, his father, John COTTON I, was 26 and his mother, Isabella Fauconer, was 23. He married Baroness Joan De Venables in 1431, in Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 1 daughter. He died on 22 May 1455, in St Albans, Hertfordshire, England, at the age of 47.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Richard Cotton est né en 1408, à Hamstall Ridware, Staffordshire, Angleterre, son père, John COTTON I, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Isabella Fauconer, avait 23 ans. Il a épousé Baroness Joan De Venables en 1431, à Staffordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé le 22 mai 1455, à St Albans, Hertfordshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 47 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Richard Cotton 1408 in Hamstall Ridware, Staffordshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater John COTTON I 26 und seine Mutter Isabella Fauconer 23. Er hat 1431 in Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom Baroness Joan De Venables geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb am 22. Mai 1455 im Alter von 47 Jahren in St Albans, Hertfordshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Richard Cotton è nato il nel 1408 nel Hamstall Ridware, Staffordshire, England, il suo padre, John COTTON I, aveva 26 anni e il suo madre, Isabella Fauconer, aveva 23 anni. Lui sposato Baroness Joan De Venables nel 1431, nel Staffordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 1 figlia. Morì il 22 maggio 1455, nel St Albans, Hertfordshire, England, all'età di 47 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Richard Cottonが1408年にイギリススタフォードシュアイアハムスタル リドワーで生まれた時，父John COTTON Iは26歳，母Isabella Fauconerは23歳であった。1431年にイギリスイギリススタフォードシュアイアでBaroness Joan De Venablesと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1455年5月22日にイギリスハートフォードシュアイアスト アルバンスで47歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Richard Cotton(이)가 1408년 영국 스태퍼드실르 함스탤 리드워에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John COTTON I(은)는 26세였고, 그의 어머니 Isabella Fauconer(은)는 23세였다. 그는 1431년 영국 잉글랜드 스태퍼드실르에서 Baroness Joan De Venables(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1455년 5월 22일 영국 허트퍼드실르 스트 알반스에서 47세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Richard Cotton nasceu em 1408, em Hamstall Ridware, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, John COTTON I, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Isabella Fauconer, tinha 23 anos. Ele casou-se com Baroness Joan De Venables em 1431, em Staffordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 22 de maio de 1455, em St Albans, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra, com 47 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Richard Cotton родился в 1408 г. в местности хамсталл ридвар, стаффордшир, Англия, его отцу по имени John COTTON I было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Isabella Fauconer, 23. Он женился на женщине по имени Baroness Joan De Venables в 1431 г. в местности стаффордшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер 22 мая 1455 г. в местности ст албанс, хиртфордшир, Англия в возрасте 47 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir Richard Cotton nació en 1408, en Hamstall Ridware, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, su papá, John COTTON I, tenía 26 años y su mamá, Isabella Fauconer, tenía 23 años. Se casó con Baroness Joan De Venables en 1431, en Staffordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 1 hija. Murió el 22 de mayo de 1455, en St Albans, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 47 años.
